jobs.description: I’m looking for someone experienced with Shopify to run a CRO audit on my store. We’re getting great traffic, but I feel like there’s a lot of room to improve conversion. I want someone who can go through the full user journey, understand where people are dropping off, and identify what’s actually hurting performance. This isn’t just a design review — I’m looking for real insights based on behavior, data, and experience with ecom. The goal is to review things like product pages, overall UX, and the funnel, and come back with clear, actionable suggestions to improve conversion. The final deliverable would be a solid report with recommendations I can actually implement. If you’ve done this before and can show real results, that’s a big plus.

jobs.description: I am the founder of God-First Sport Wears — a faith-driven athletic apparel brand newly launched on Shopify. Our brand is built on purpose: blending performance sportswear with a bold spiritual identity that resonates deeply with our audience. We are seeking a skilled Klaviyo Email Marketing Specialist to build our Welcome Flow from the ground up — a fully automated, conversion-optimized email sequence that turns new subscribers into loyal first-time buyers. What We Need Built 01 Klaviyo + Shopify full integration & account audit 02 5–7 email Welcome Series flow (automated sequence) 03 Branded email templates aligned with our identity 04 Compelling, on-brand email copy for every email 05 Segmentation logic: subscriber intent & behavior splits 06 Flow testing, QA, and live deployment Who We're Looking For Demonstrated Klaviyo experience (portfolio or live flow examples required) Proven Shopify + Klaviyo integration experience Skilled in email copywriting — brand voice matters deeply to us Understanding of DTC brand storytelling and faith/lifestyle niches is a strong plus Ability to set up conditional splits, filters, and smart send timing Excellent communication — we expect proactive updates throughout

jobs.description: We are a growing breastfeeding cover brand selling to customers across the UK. We are looking for a reliable and warm customer service representative to join our small team on a part-time basis. What you will be doing: - Replying to customer emails daily in a warm, professional tone (we have templates and a full process guide to help you) - Checking order statuses in Shopify and updating customers - Managing returns and refunds following our step-by-step process - Handling delivery enquiries, tracking updates and failed deliveries - Communicating with our fulfilment team for address changes or cancellations - Sending a daily summary of open refund and return cases - Helping prevent chargebacks by responding quickly and professionally What we are looking for: - Excellent written English - Previous e-commerce customer service experience - Comfortable using Shopify or similar platforms - Reliable, self-motivated and able to work independently - Available during UK business hours (Monday to Friday) - Patient and empathetic when dealing with difficult customers We move fast as a team and care deeply about every customer interaction. If you are someone who takes pride in their work and loves helping people, we would love to hear from you. Please include the phrase warm mellow somewhere in your application so we know you have read this far. We will provide you with a full email flow guide, templates for every situation, and clear processes so you can get started quickly. This is a great opportunity for someone who is organised, a strong communicator and passionate about delivering great customer service. Any organic growth results you've achieved Thanks for applying!

jobs.description: We are looking for a reliable, detail-oriented Customer Support Agent to join Court Four, a fast-growing premium lifestyle activewear brand rooted in the global padel culture. Court Four is built around the idea of moving seamlessly from court to café, studio, and city, premium activewear designed for women who want to look elevated throughout every moment of their day. We are not a discount brand or a generic activewear store. We are building a warm, premium, lifestyle-first brand with a strong focus on customer experience, trust, and long-term growth. As we continue to grow, we want to bring in someone who can help us deliver a smooth, professional, and warm customer experience. What you will do Your main responsibility will be handling customer support emails in English. This includes: Responding to customer questions about orders, shipping, returns, exchanges, sizing, and products Handling customer conversations in a clear, kind, and professional tone Processing and managing chargebacks when needed Communicating with our supplier about orders, stock, shipping issues, and customer cases Keeping track of support cases in Google Sheets and/or Notion Updating internal notes so the team always knows what is happening Helping with small operational tasks when needed Most of the work will be customer support, but we are looking for someone flexible who is also open to helping with small additional tasks outside the strict support scope. Who we are looking for We are looking for someone who is: Fluent in written English Clear, professional, and warm in communication Reliable and structured Detail-oriented Comfortable working with Google Sheets Comfortable using Notion or willing to learn it Able to communicate with suppliers clearly Able to handle difficult customer situations calmly Proactive and willing to think along with the business Available for a long-term collaboration Can work with standard templates for responses, and does this quickly. Experience with Shopify, Gmail, chargebacks, returns, or e-commerce customer support is a must. Why work with us Court Four is still early in its journey, but we are growing quickly and building something with real brand potential. You will not just be answering random support tickets, you will become part of the operational backbone of a premium lifestyle brand. We value people who are reliable, thoughtful, and want to grow with the company. If the collaboration goes well, this can become a long-term role with more responsibility over time. Important Please start your application with the word court so we know you read the full job post. In your application, please include: Your previous customer support experience Whether you have worked with Shopify before Whether you have experience with chargebacks Your experience with Google Sheets and Notion Your availability per week Why you think you would be a good fit for Court Four We are looking for someone who can help us create the kind of customer experience that matches a premium brand: warm, clear, professional, and trustworthy.
jobs.title: Customer Support Agent for Fast-Growing Premium Activewear Brand

jobs.description: About Us Jod-Z is a premium girls' legwear brand, a specialist, not a generalist. We make high-performance leggings with silicone knee grips, squat-proof fabric, and secure pockets designed for girls who move. We've built a loyal customer base through organic traffic alone and we're now ready to scale. We have a clear vision and we need the right person to bring it to life on our Shopify store. The Vision: The Crossover Angle This is the heart of everything we are building. Jod-Z cannot just be horse riding leggings. The opportunity in front of us is to position Jod-Z as the go-to premium performance legwear brand for active girls, from the stable to the schoolyard, from the sports pitch to the playground, from pony club to casual Saturdays. This crossover angle is not a small tweak to the website. It is a complete shift in how we communicate, how we show the product, and who we are speaking to. Every single touchpoint on the site needs to reflect this expanded identity. A parent landing on our page should instantly understand that these leggings work for riding, yes, but also for every other part of her daughter's active life. That realisation is what justifies the premium price point and what turns a visitor into a buyer. We believe this crossover positioning is what will unlock serious growth. Getting it right on the website is the foundation everything else is built on. The Opportunity Our current conversion rate is significantly below industry standard and we know exactly why. This isn't a traffic problem. It's a persuasion problem. The copy is generic, imagery is flat, CTAs are inconsistent, and the crossover lifestyle angle is entirely absent. A specialist who fixes every touchpoint and truly understands and champions this crossover vision could realistically deliver a major revenue increase without increasing traffic. Key Deliverables Homepage repositioning. The hero section must lead with the crossover angle immediately. A parent who has never heard of horse riding leggings should land on this page and think "these are made for my daughter." New direction: "Ride. Run. Play. Premium legwear designed for every adventure." The crossover message must be unmissable from the first scroll. Product page overhaul. Transform bullet-point feature lists into benefit-led copy that speaks to every type of active girl, not just riders. Address real parent anxieties around slipping, transparency, washability, and value. The product descriptions should make a parent feel that these leggings belong in every part of her daughter's week. Visual integration strategy. The site currently lives entirely in the stable. We need it to live everywhere. Advise on and implement lifestyle imagery covering school runs, playground, sports training, and casual wear alongside the riding context. Each product colour should anchor to a different lifestyle moment, making the crossover feel natural and intentional rather than forced. UX and CTA standardisation. Audit and fix all CTAs site-wide. Replace any confusing text with clear, consistent actions: "Add to Cart," "Buy Now," "Shop Leggings." Streamline navigation and checkout flow so nothing interrupts a parent who is ready to buy. "Beyond the Saddle" section. This is a core piece of the crossover strategy. Design and build a dedicated homepage section or collection page that speaks directly to non-equestrian active girls covering dance, athletics, casual wear, and outdoor play. This section should feel like a natural extension of the brand, not an afterthought. Messaging: "Built for busy girls. Approved by busy parents." Ad-ready site cohesion. We will be driving Instagram and Facebook traffic shortly. Every page a paid visitor lands on must consistently reflect the crossover angle. The messaging, visuals, and flow must feel seamless from ad click to checkout. Must-Have Skills Proven Shopify experience with theme customisation, Liquid templating, and performance optimisation. Strong copywriting instinct for e-commerce that is benefit-led, conversion-focused, and brand-voice consistent. A UX and CRO background where you understand why users drop off and how to fix it. Experience with lifestyle, fashion, or children's product brands. The ability to audit an existing store and deliver a structured improvement plan, not just execute tasks. Attention to detail across every touchpoint. Nice to Have Experience with Meta ad-ready landing pages, familiarity with AI image generation tools, and previous CRO case studies with measurable conversion results. Phased Timeline Week 1: Full site audit. Rewrite homepage, collection pages, all product descriptions, and standardise every CTA with the crossover angle running through all of it. Week 2: Integrate lifestyle imagery and implement the riding and activewear crossover visuals throughout the site. Week 3: Launch the "Beyond the Saddle" section, complete the final pre-ads check, and hand off. To Apply, Please Include Two or three Shopify stores you have worked on, with before and after context where possible. One example of product copy you rewrote and the impact it had. Your read on our current site and how you would bring the crossover angle to life. Your proposed approach and rough timeline. Your fixed price or hourly rate for this project. Brand Direction for Reference Jod-Z: "Performance legwear for girls who do it all." We are not competing with cheap leggings. We are the specialist brand solving slipping, transparency, poor fit, lack of pockets, and lack of style in one premium product. Our expanded world covers riding, pony club, school runs, sports, playgrounds, and everyday fashion. The parent buying psychology centres on safety, durability, value, confidence, and washability. If a parent thinks "she'll live in these" we've won.
jobs.title: Shopify Developer & CRO Specialist

jobs.description: Key Deliverables: Design: Full Impulse theme customization, engaging homepage with high-quality banners, and 100% mobile/tablet responsiveness. Inventory: Organized product collections, detailed descriptions, and seamless navigation menus. Functionality: Full UX setup (Cart, Checkout, Accounts), secure payment gateways, shipping config, and domain connection. Conversion: Trust badges, urgency apps, abandoned cart recovery, and email capture popups. Engagement: Live chat, social media integration, and automated email marketing setup. Post-Purchase: Order tracking system and professional legal/essential pages. Performance: Fast loading speeds and basic on-page SEO. Required: Please provide a walkthrough video and post-launch support.
jobs.title: Looking for a skilled Shopify Developer to create an e-commerce site for my women's fashion brand.

jobs.description: For our DROPSHIPPING business, we are seeking for dedicated customer service agents. We are looking for agents that have experience in Shopify, Reamaze and the DROPSHIPPING customer behavior. This remote role demands excellent communication skills and a strong commitment to providing outstanding customer support. The ideal candidate will be responsible for handling customer inquiries, processing orders, and resolving issues efficiently via email. RESPONSIBILITIES - Respond to all customer inquiries in a timely and accurate manner - Documenting the refunds or any specific complaints - Handling customer complaints - Be focused on customer care - Being able to escalate issues to the managers - Demonstrate a high level of professionalism at all times. MUST HAVE KNOWLEDGE OF THESE PLATFORMS: - Shopify (eCommerce platform) - Reamaze (support help desk) - Google Spreadsheet (for documentation) - Slack/Discord (communication channel) - DeepL (for translation) APPLICANT NEEDS TO - Be available for 40 hours per immediate - Speak fluent English both written and verbally - Minimum 1 year of experience in CS - Be available for weekly team calls - Must be organized, motivated, and willing to learn and grow. - Available to join immediately - Have experience in dropshipping Please send your resume, cover letter, and any relevant work samples. Use “APPLE” as the subject line of your email.
jobs.title: Customer Service Agent | Shopify | Dropshipping

jobs.description: ⚠ READ THIS BEFORE YOU APPLY We are hiring multiple candidates — but we are extremely selective about who joins. Do not apply if: - You're looking for quick, easy money with minimal effort - You need constant supervision or hand-holding to do your job - You treat this as a side hustle or backup income stream - You disappear when things get busy or difficult - You're not prepared to own your results — good or bad - You're not in this for the long term We move fast, we hold high standards, and we don't have time to manage people who aren't fully committed. If that sounds intimidating — this role is not for you. If it sounds like exactly where you thrive — keep reading. About us We are a fast-growing backend operations partner for e-commerce brands and dropshipping stores. We don't just "handle support" — we take full ownership of the backend. From customer support and disputes to logistics, systems, and reporting — we make sure brands can scale without operational chaos. Why people love working here: - We're a young, driven team that genuinely invests in the people who show up. Strong performers move up fast — we promote from within, always. - You're not a cog here; your decisions have real impact on real businesses. Expect good energy, open communication, and zero corporate nonsense. And working hands-on with 6–7 figure stores will teach you more about e-commerce operations than any course ever could. - If you're ambitious and want to build a real career in e-commerce operations — this is the place to do it. Long-term opportunities This is not a dead-end job. Managers who perform well grow into senior roles, head of department positions (support, logistics, reporting, QA), and strategic roles as we continue to scale and build out our systems. We're building something serious — the people who grow with us now will have a real stake in where this goes. What you'll be responsible for As a Manager, you will oversee the full backend operations for multiple e-commerce stores. Your role includes: - Leading and managing a team of customer support agents - Monitoring ticket quality, response times, and overall performance - Ensuring smooth daily operations across all assigned stores - Handling escalations, complex cases, and problem-solving - Managing refunds, returns, and customer issues - Overseeing chargebacks and PayPal disputes - Communicating directly with clients via Slack - Monitoring orders, shipping, and supplier communication - Identifying operational bottlenecks and improving systems - Preparing daily and weekly reports with insights and recommendations - You are not just managing tasks — you are responsible for the performance and stability of the entire backend. Who we're looking for - Takes full ownership — no excuses, no finger-pointing - Proactive and solution-oriented by default - Excellent written communication (clear, structured, professional) - Reliable and consistent — day in, day out - Comfortable leading a team and giving direct feedback - Spots problems before they escalate and comes with solutions - Fast learner who actively seeks to improve To apply Send a message that includes: - Your experience in e-commerce / customer support - Any experience managing teams - Tools you've worked with (e.g. Shopify, Gorgias, etc.) - Why you think you're a good fit for this role Applications without a proper introduction will not be considered.
jobs.title: E-commerce Customer Support Manager Needed — MULTIPLE POSITIONS OPEN

jobs.description: Shopify eCommerce Operations Manager We are looking for a highly capable Shopify eCommerce Operations Manager to take ownership of the daily operations across two established online furniture stores. This is a hands-on execution role focused on keeping the business running efficiently, profitably, and without friction. You will work directly with the founder and take responsibility for orders, suppliers, product data, and store performance. Key Responsibilities 1. Order & Supplier Management Process and manage customer orders end-to-end Coordinate with multiple suppliers (stock, lead times, fulfilment) Handle delays, returns, and issue resolution 2. Product & Catalogue Management Manage and optimise a large Shopify catalogue (thousands of SKUs) Update product data (pricing, margins, descriptions) Ensure accuracy across all listings 3. Google Shopping & Feeds Manage product feeds via tools like Simprosys Google Shopping Feed Monitor product performance and flag issues Assist in improving feed quality and structure 4. Shipping & Logistics Maintain shipping rules and pricing (postcode-based systems) Coordinate deliveries and freight providers Improve delivery accuracy and cost control 5. Store Optimisation Improve collections, navigation, and product presentation Identify conversion issues and recommend improvements Maintain a clean, premium storefront 6. Reporting & Execution Weekly reporting: Sales performance Product performance Key issues and actions Maintain clear task tracking and follow-through 🔹 Requirements Strong experience with Shopify (essential) Experience managing large product catalogues (1,000+ SKUs) Familiarity with Google Merchant Center and Shopping feeds Highly organised with strong attention to detail Able to take ownership and complete tasks without supervision Strong communication and problem-solving skills 🔹 Hours & Pay 20–40 hours per week Preferred working hours: 11am–3pm (Sydney time overlap) $8–$10 USD per hour (based on experience) 🔹 What Success Looks Like Orders processed accurately and on time Suppliers managed proactively Product data clean and optimised Store runs smoothly without constant input Problems are identified and solved early 🔹 Important This is not a basic VA role. We are looking for someone who: Takes ownership Thinks ahead Solves problems independently 🔹 Application Instructions Please include: Your Shopify experience (stores managed, size of catalogue) Experience with Google Shopping or product feeds Examples of operational improvements you have made Your availability and hourly rate (USD)
jobs.title: Shopify eCommerce Operations Manager

jobs.description: Hey Kim! I came across your profile and I’m currently looking for a reliable customer support agent for my dropshipping store. At the moment, I run one Shopify store, but my goal is to scale this into multiple stores over time. That’s why I’m looking for someone long-term — someone who wants to grow with the business and become a stable part of the team. We receive daily customer emails regarding order status, delays, returns, and general questions. I’m looking for someone who can handle these professionally, clearly, and with empathy, so customers feel heard and supported. What I’m looking for: Fast response time (preferably within 1–2 hours during working hours) Experience with Shopify and dropshipping (this is very important) Ability to handle refunds, complaints, and order-related issues independently Strong communication skills in English Ability to follow templates, but also think independently when needed It’s important that you already understand how e-commerce customer support works — including tracking issues, returns, and communicating with customers in a calm and professional way. I’m not looking for short-term help. I want to work with someone consistently and build a long-term working relationship. If you think you’re a good fit, please let me know: Your availability (hours per day/week) Your experience with Shopify/dropshipping How you would handle a delayed order where tracking hasn’t updated for 7 days Looking forward to your response! — Gerben Wagendorp
jobs.title: Customer Support Agent for Shopify Dropshipping Store (Long-Term)

jobs.description: ⚠ READ THIS BEFORE YOU APPLY We are hiring multiple candidates — but we are extremely selective about who joins. Do not apply if: - You're looking for easy, repetitive work without thinking - You copy-paste replies without understanding the problem - You need constant supervision to stay productive - You disappear when workload increases - You are slow, careless, or inconsistent - You don’t take responsibility for mistakes - You’re not serious about improving your skills We work fast, we expect accuracy, and we rely on people who can be trusted without being watched. If that feels like pressure — this role is not for you. If that feels like the standard you want — keep reading. About us We are a fast-growing backend operations partner for e-commerce brands and dropshipping stores. - We don’t just “reply to emails.” - We manage the full backend — customer support, logistics issues, disputes, and reporting — so brands can scale without chaos. - Every ticket you handle is part of a bigger system. - We don’t just solve problems — we prevent them. Why people like working here - We’re a young, fast-moving team that rewards people who perform. - If you’re reliable, sharp, and consistent, you’ll grow fast. - Most of our managers started as agents. - You’ll work on real stores doing 6–7 figures — and you’ll learn how e-commerce actually works behind the scenes. - No corporate structure. No micromanagement. - Just high standards and real responsibility. What you’ll be responsible for As a Customer Support Agent, your role is to execute at a high level — every day. You will: - Handle customer emails quickly and accurately - Work inside Gorgias and Gmail to manage tickets - Assist customers with orders, tracking, refunds, and issues - Follow SOPs and brand guidelines in every reply - Maintain fast response and resolution times - Document issues and keep sheets updated - Track orders and flag delays or problems - Escalate complex or sensitive cases when needed - Keep the inbox clean and organized This is not just “replying to emails.” You are responsible for the customer experience and backend stability. Who we’re looking for - Fast, accurate, and detail-oriented - Strong written English (clear, structured, professional) - Consistent — you show up and perform every day - Follows systems and SOPs without shortcuts - Thinks before acting — no guessing - Takes ownership and learns from mistakes - Communicates clearly when something is unclear - Handles pressure and workload without dropping quality Requirements - Experience in e-commerce customer support - Experience with Shopify - Experience with Gorgias and Gmail - Ability to meet KPIs and response time targets - Strong communication and problem-solving skills We look at: - Communication quality - Speed and accuracy - SOP adherence - Reliability and consistency Strong performers grow fast — including opportunities to increase rates and move into more senior roles over time. To apply Send a message that includes: - Your experience in e-commerce customer support - Tools you’ve worked with (Shopify, Gorgias, etc.) - Examples of how you handle customer issues - Why you think you’re a good fit Applications without a proper introduction will not be considered.

jobs.description: We’re building a Shopify store for eva-alert.com and are looking for a designer to create high-quality wireframes based on our existing brand style guide. The goal is to take inspiration from Apple’s clean, minimal design approach (layout, spacing, hierarchy—not copying) and apply it to our brand. You’ll work from: Our style guide (colors, typography, components) Screenshots/references from Apple’s website and/or our Shopify template Our product and content direction What we need: We want structured, section-based wireframes (low to mid fidelity is fine, but must be clean and thoughtful) for the following pages: Homepage (hero-focused, storytelling sections) Collection pages Product Detail Page (PDP) Blog / content page Scope of work: Translate references into original wireframes aligned with our brand. Design modular, Shopify-friendly sections (think reusable blocks) Focus on layout, spacing, hierarchy, and UX flow Ensure consistency across all pages Deliver in Figma (preferred) Important: This is NOT a full visual design job—we already have a style guide. We need someone who can: Think in systems and layouts Work with constraints Capture a premium, Apple-like structure and experience Ideal candidate: Strong experience with Shopify UX and eCommerce flows Skilled in wireframing and layout systems Good eye for clean, modern, minimal design Comfortable working from references without copying Experience with brands like Apple, Notion, Stripe, etc. is a plus To apply, please include: Examples of wireframes or UX work (especially eCommerce) A brief note on how you approach translating inspiration into original layouts Your availability and estimated timeline
jobs.title: Job Title: Shopify Wireframe Designer (Apple-Inspired Style, Based on Existing Style Guide)

jobs.description: We are The Butterfly Effect a brand expanding into the Spanish / Latin American market and we need someone who can take our Shopify store and ship it translated, configured, and ready to sell in one project. This is a hybrid role: native-level Spanish translator + Shopify specialist. We need both in one person, because handing it off between a translator and a Shopify ops freelancer always ends in a mess. What you'd own (one project, fixed scope): 1. Shopify Markets setup - Configure Spain as a new market (currency, language, domain/subfolder strategy) - Set up Shopify Translate & Adapt — but don't lean on machine translation - Shipping zones, rates, and carrier configuration for Spain 2. Payment gateways - Activate and test the right mix for Spanish buyers (where relevant) - Configure local payment methods we'd otherwise miss - Test full checkout end-to-end in Spanish, on mobile 3. Translation (native-quality, not Google Translate) - Full theme/store UI (navigation, footer, checkout, account pages) - All product titles + descriptions (we'll tell you which collections are priority) - Policies (returns, shipping, terms, privacy) — we'll provide source - Marketing copy needs to feel like it was written by a Spanish copywriter, not translated 4. QA + handoff - Mobile + desktop in Spanish - Loom walkthrough showing what you set up and how to maintain it What we're NOT looking for: - Pure translators with no Shopify Markets experience - Shopify devs who'll outsource the translation to Google or someone else. - Anyone whose first instinct is "I'll just use Translate & Adapt's auto-translate and call it done" - Generic "I localize websites" — we want Spain-specific knowledge (Bizum exists, IVA matters, return culture is different) Who you are: - Native or near-native Spanish (Spain — we'll discuss if you're Latin American but writing for Spain) - 2+ years Shopify experience, including Shopify Markets specifically - Have launched at least one DTC store in Spain before (we'll ask for proof) - Comfortable with payment gateway setup, tax configuration, and theme translation - Strong copywriting feel — fashion / lifestyle ideal Bonus: - Have done English ⇄ Spanish localization for fashion or beauty brands - Familiar with Klaviyo (we'll need email templates ported too) - Can advise on Spanish PR, influencer or affiliate landscape (not required, but a plus) - POV on Spanish ecommerce trends in 2026 Logistics: - Fixed-price project: budget €2,500-5,000 depending on scope (we'll align after a discovery call) - Timeline: 3-4 weeks from kickoff to live - Optional retainer after launch: €30-60/hour for ongoing product translations and updates as we add SKUs - Async work — Loom updates, Notion brief, Slack for urgent To apply: 1. Send a 60-90 second Loom: introduce yourself + show ONE Shopify store you've localized for Spain. What did you set up, what was tricky, what would you do differently. 2. In 1 sentence: what's the most common mistake non-Spanish brands make when localizing for Spain? 3. Confirm: are you a native Spain Spanish speaker, or Latin American? (Both can work, but be honest.) We respond to everyone who follows the format. Skip cover letters.
jobs.title: Shopify plus expert + Spanish translator — set up Latin American market end-to-end for DTC brand

jobs.description: Australian BAS / GST Setup Specialist (Initial Nil Return + Quarterly Compliance) Job Description: New Zealand-based ecommerce business recently registered for: ABN Australian GST I need an Australian BAS / GST specialist to help submit BAS filings for GST as I cannot do this from New Zealand. Immediate Need: Lodge initial March BAS nil return correctly Confirm ATO / GST setup is correct Ensure quarterly BAS returns are properly structured going forward Ongoing: Quarterly BAS lodgement support/advice Basic GST compliance for ecommerce business Ensure no setup mistakes early on Business: Shopify ecommerce NZ company expanding into Australia Currently early stage, low complexity Preparing for future stock imports, but immediate priority is BAS compliance Looking For: Australian BAS / GST experience Experience with small business / ecommerce preferred Practical, simple guidance (not overcomplicated) Goal: Make sure I start correctly now, file the March nil return properly, and have a clear quarterly BAS process moving forward.

jobs.description: Description: I need an experienced schema markup developer to fix, update and expand the JSON-LD structured data on my custom PHP eCommerce website at exoticcarparts.com. This is a parts retailer specializing in Rolls-Royce and Bentley automotive parts. This is NOT a WordPress or Shopify site. It is a custom PHP CMS. You must have experience with custom PHP websites. PART 1 — URGENT FIXES 1. Remove fake aggregateRating A previous developer added fake 5-star ratings to all product schema. This violates Google's structured data policies. Please remove the aggregateRating and review fields from ALL product pages site-wide immediately. This is the highest priority task. 2. Update priceValidUntil Change all product schema priceValidUntil dates from 2025-12-31 to 2027-12-31 across all product listings site-wide. 3. Fix Organisation schema postcode In the global Organisation JSON-LD schema that appears on every page change: "postalCode": "11212" to "postalCode": "11413" 4. Fix styles.css MIME type error Currently returning text/html instead of text/css — please fix server configuration. 5. Fix Tawk.to CORS error Live chat script is being blocked by CORS policy — please update embed code to latest version. 6. Fix FancyBox duplicate initialization FancyBox is loading twice on every page — please remove duplicate script include. PART 2 — NEW SCHEMA IMPLEMENTATION 7. BreadcrumbList Schema Implement BreadcrumbList schema on all product and category pages. Pages already display breadcrumbs visually — we need the schema to match so Google shows them in search results. 8. FAQPage Schema Implement FAQPage schema on all main catalogue pages — for example: Rolls-Royce Silver Dawn parts page Rolls-Royce Cullinan parts page Bentley Continental parts page Bentley Mulsanne parts page Each page should have 3 to 5 relevant FAQ questions and answers about the parts, fitment and shipping. 9. LocalBusiness Schema Replace the current Organisation schema with a full LocalBusiness schema including: Business name: Exotic Car Parts USA Address: 182-21 150th Avenue, Springfield Gardens, Queens, NY 11413 Phone: ------------------------------------------- Email: ---------------------------------------------------- Opening hours: 7am to 8pm, 7 days a week Business type: AutoPartsStore Geographic coordinates Service area: United States 10. Improved Offer Schema on all product pages Update existing product Offer schema to include: seller field identifying Exotic Car Parts USA sku field using the part number category field: Vehicle Parts & Accessories 11. Vehicle Schema Where possible implement Vehicle schema to describe the fitment relationship between parts and the vehicles they fit — for example linking a brake pad listing to the specific Bentley or Rolls-Royce model it fits. 12. Validate all schema After all changes please run a representative sample of pages through Google's Rich Results Test and Schema.org Validator and provide a report confirming no errors remain. BEFORE APPLYING PLEASE CONFIRM: You have experience with custom PHP websites — NOT WordPress or Shopify You have experience with JSON-LD schema markup You have experience with Apache and cPanel server configuration You can complete all tasks within 5 days Deliverables: All urgent fixes completed All new schema types implemented site-wide Validation report showing clean results Brief summary of all changes made
jobs.title: Fix & Implement Advanced JSON-LD Schema Markup on Custom PHP eCommerce Website

jobs.description: eCommerce Customer Service & Community Executive - Korean and English Location : Remote Type : Full-Time Salary : RM4500 - RM5500 *Probation period: 3 months Platform Focus: Instagram & Discord, eCommerce (Shopify) About Us HARUMIO is a fast-growing eCommerce platform dedicated to anything and everything Korean. We believe in building more than just a brand; we build a community. With a strong focus on Instagram for brand discovery and Discord for community connection, we strive to deliver an engaging, human, and rapid support experience to our users. Job Summary We are looking for a POLYGLOT, SPEAK/READ/WRITE IN ENGLISH AND KOREAN LANGUAGE, who is a proactive, tech-savvy Customer Service & Community Executive to be the face of our brand on Discord and Instagram. You will not only resolve customer queries regarding orders, shipping, and returns but also cultivate a positive, engaging atmosphere on our Discord server and manage our Instagram DMs/comments. The ideal candidate is comfortable with social media, loves chatting with people, and thrives in a fast-paced digital environment. Key Responsibilities 1. Discord Community Moderation & Support (60%) Active Engagement: Monitor and engage in Discord chat channels, fostering a welcoming atmosphere for new and existing members. Ticket Support: Manage incoming support tickets using Discord integration tools (e.g., Ticket Tool) to resolve customer order issues. Moderation: Enforce community guidelines to ensure a respectful environment, managing bots, and handling potential conflicts. Events & Updates: Support in hosting community events and disseminating company announcements, product drops, or updates to the server. Feedback Loop: Relay community sentiment and product feedback from Discord to the internal team. 2. Instagram DM & Social Engagement (30%) DM Management: Respond to customer questions via Instagram DMs, providing support on order tracking, product recommendations, and returns. Comment Monitoring: Monitor and reply to public comments on posts, reels, and stories in a prompt, friendly manner. Story Engagement: Engage with user-generated content (mentions, story tags) to build brand loyalty. 3. General E-commerce Support (10%) Backend Coordination: Coordinate with warehouse/shipping teams to resolve delivery issues. Record Keeping: Update systems (Shopify) with interaction logs from social channels. Required Skills & Qualifications Min. 3 years customer service platform expertise: Proven experience using Discord (server management/moderation) and Instagram (business accounts/DMs). Communication: Excellent written communication skills, with the ability to maintain a friendly, on-brand "human" voice. Problem-Solving: Strong ability to troubleshoot problems independently and think on your feet. E-commerce Savvy: Previous experience in eCommerce support (order management) is a plus. Empathy & Patience: Ability to manage difficult customers or high-volume chats with calmness. Time Management: Highly organized with the ability to multitask across different platforms. Work Schedule Working days: Monday - Sunday Rotational 5 days/week (including weekend and public holidays) Working hours: Rotational Shift (9am - 6pm, 3pm - 12am) (9 hour shifts including break/day)
jobs.title: eCommerce Customer Service & Community Executive - Korean and English

jobs.description: Looking for an Shopify expert to help us improving Judge.me Review Request rate and update our template. I need a clean, high-converting HTML email template built for Judge.me (Shopify review app). What I need: • Single HTML file, inline CSS only (Gmail compatible) • Mobile-first, 600px max width • Dynamic merge tags for customer name, product image, product title, and review URL • Clickable star rating buttons (1–5) that pre-load the review form at the selected rating • One strong CTA button • Clean, modern design that feels personal — not corporate • Matching reminder email (slightly shorter version) Ideal candidate: • Has built HTML emails before (show me examples) • Knows email client limitations (Gmail, Apple Mail, Outlook) • Familiar with Shopify or Judge.me is a bonus but not required This is a small, focused project. jobs.title: Shopify expert to optimise review request app (judge.me)

jobs.description: Compensation: $1,200–$2,000/month depending on experience + performance-based bonuses tied to conversion rate / AOV improvements Growth Path: - Increased compensation based on results - Opportunity to expand into broader DTC growth role - Long-term position as we scale Shopify aggressively ---------------------------------------- About Us ---------------------------------------- Tuff Pupper is a leading dog accessories brand doing ~$650k/month on Amazon with double-digit growth. We are now building out our Shopify (DTC) channel seriously. We already have a working Shopify site. We are NOT looking for a redesign. We are looking to improve and optimize what already exists. ---------------------------------------- Role Structure ---------------------------------------- This is a part-time role with flexible hours. Expected workload: - Approximately 15–25 hours per week depending on initiatives We care about results and improvements, not time tracking. ---------------------------------------- What We Need (Important) ---------------------------------------- We want someone who can take initiative and improve our site without needing constant direction. This includes: - Identifying opportunities to increase conversion rate - Improving average order value (bundles, upsells, cross-sells) - Enhancing customer experience - Recommending and implementing the right apps/tools You should tell us: - What should be improved - Why it matters - What it will look like when done - How it will impact revenue ---------------------------------------- Role Overview ---------------------------------------- This is NOT a task-based role. You are expected to: - Identify problems and opportunities - Propose solutions - Execute improvements - Measure results ---------------------------------------- Core Responsibilities ---------------------------------------- 1. Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) - Improve product pages (layout, structure, trust elements) - Optimize mobile experience - Reduce friction in the buying process 2. AOV Growth (Critical) - Implement bundles (e.g., harness + leash sets) - “Shop the look” / product sets - Upsells and cross-sells - Cart and post-purchase offers 3. App & Feature Implementation - Recommend and install high-impact Shopify apps - Avoid unnecessary tools - Ensure clean integration with existing site 4. Landing Pages (Email Support) - Build simple, high-converting landing pages for Klaviyo campaigns - Align with promotions and product pushes 5. Continuous Improvement - Identify new opportunities regularly - Run small tests and iterate - Focus on revenue impact, not just design ---------------------------------------- Important Constraints ---------------------------------------- - DO NOT redesign the site - DO NOT overcomplicate with too many apps - Focus on improvements, not rebuilding ---------------------------------------- Bonus (Not Required) ---------------------------------------- - Experience in pet products / pet accessories - Experience in outdoor gear, tactical gear, or athletic products - Experience working with multi-product catalogs (bundles, sets, accessories) Additional bonus: - You are a dog owner or familiar with dog products These are helpful but not required. We prioritize strong CRO thinking and ability to drive results. ---------------------------------------- What Success Looks Like ---------------------------------------- - Conversion rate improves over time - Average order value increases - Site feels cleaner, easier to use, more convincing - Clear revenue impact from changes ---------------------------------------- Important ---------------------------------------- You must be self-directed. We are not looking to manage day-to-day tasks. You are expected to: - Identify what matters - Propose actions - Execute efficiently ---------------------------------------- To Apply ---------------------------------------- Please include: 1. 2–3 Shopify stores you have improved 2. Specific results (conversion rate, AOV, revenue impact) 3. Your expected monthly rate and why 4. Answer the following: A) What are 3 specific improvements you would make to a pet accessories Shopify store? B) How would you increase AOV for a brand selling harnesses, leashes, toys, and apparel? C) What apps/tools would you consider for bundles or “shop the look” and why? D) Give one example of a CRO improvement you made and the result ---------------------------------------- Hiring Process ---------------------------------------- Shortlisted candidates may be given a small paid task: - Review our site and propose 3 improvements with expected impact
jobs.title: Shopify CRO & Growth Operator (Pet Accessories / High-Growth Brand)

All listings will be created directly inside TikTok Shop. Please start your proposal with the word “candy” so I know you read this. jobs.description: We are looking for an experienced accessibility expert or agency to help bring our Shopify website closer to WCAG compliance standards. This is NOT a basic plugin installation project. We are looking for a team with real accessibility experience, proper testing workflow, and the ability to provide a detailed final report. Scope of Work * Full accessibility audit of our Shopify website * Identify WCAG issues across desktop and mobile * Fix accessibility issues directly on the website * Manual + automated testing * Cross-browser testing * Keyboard navigation testing * Screen reader compatibility review * Final accessibility report/documentation after completion Requirements Please apply ONLY if you: * Have proven WCAG experience * Have worked on Shopify websites before * Can provide examples or case studies * Understand accessibility best practices beyond overlays/plugins * Can complete the project within 2 weeks When applying, please include: 1. Your estimated timeline 2. Estimated total pricing 3. Your testing process/workflow 4. Examples of past WCAG projects 5. What tools you use for accessibility testing 6. Whether testing is manual, automated, or both Preferred * Experience with WCAG 2.1 AA standards * Experience working with eCommerce brands * Ability to communicate clearly and provide organized reporting
jobs.title: WCAG Accessibility Expert Needed for Shopify Website (Audit + Remediation + Final Report)

jobs.description: Overview: I’ve purchased two ecommerce / AI training programs that provide step-by-step tasks to build and launch online stores. Stores are partially provided and completed. The issue is not strategy — it’s execution. I am not able to give the consistant time to this, leaving a vauable resource unused. I’m looking for someone who can reliably and consistantly follow cource instructions and complete tasks, effectively acting as the owner to move the business forward. What You’ll Do: Keep up to date with requirements and complete tasks from the structured programs. Liasse with teachers/mentors. Follow SOPs and video instructions. Use email/slack/whatsapp to communicate. Set up accounts etc as required. Report back with what was completed Flag blockers or unclear instructions that require my assistance. Maintain consistency and momentum - this is the most important part. What This Is NOT: Not a strategy or consulting role Not “tell me what to do” Requirements: Strong attention to detail - follow instructions exactly. Consistant availability, timing is not critical. Comfortable with ecommerce platforms Comfortable working with Slack, Loom. Comfortable with AI models. Good English for written communication, and understanding instructions. Ability to work independently and constructively without constant supervision Willing to learn from the provided courses and execute Nice to Have: Previous experience building ecommerce stores Familiarity with product research tools Basic copywriting skills Access & Tools: You will be given access to: Ecommerce training programs (paid courses) Store accounts and tools Dedicated email. Access will be structured and monitored for security Hours & Commitment: Ongoing role Consistant execution required. Hours unknown at present, could be 2 / day or 10-20 /week? I do not have unlimitted funds and so a weekly limit wil be in place. Flexible hours, but must complete assigned tasks on time. Whats in it for you? You’ll gain access to very expensive paid ecommerce training programs Opportunity to grow into ongoing management role if the outcome is successful. Potential for long-term work creating and managing multiple stores with profit share options. To Apply: Summary of ecommerce/store/AI experience Example of a project where you followed a system/process Your availability (days + hours per day) Final Note: This role is about consistency and execution. I am a creator who lacks time and patience for the daily tasks. I do not require a $50/hr specialist. I require an affordable solution to keep it all ticking along. If you’re someone who can deal with the small stuff and make some progress most days (even just 1 thing), be trustable with my information and reliably communicate in a professional manner - I'm waiting to hear from you :-)
